text: The first stitch of a double-armed PDS suture is placed in the right dorsolateral corner from the outside to the inside at the recipient bronchial stump.
This continuous suturing technique is easy, fast, and can also be used for vascular anastomosis.
The dorsal portion of the anastomosis is completed.

After reaching the lateral portion on the left side, the direction of the stitches can be maintained by performing one stitch from the inside to the outside and vice versa on the same side of the anastomosis.
Afterwards, the left and left anterior portion of the anastomosis completed.
The free end of the thread is used to complete the remaining circumference of the anastomosis.
Both ends are tied at the anterior aspect of the bronchus.
By establishing this technique as an institutional standard, we could lower the incidence of bronchial complications after lung transplantation to 1.56%.
